title: astragalus subalpinus
intro: Astragalus subalpinus, also known as the subalpine milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family that is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, due to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. Astragalus subalpinus has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems, cardiovascular disease, and cancer, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal medicine, Astragalus subalpinus is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or supplement to harness its medicinal properties.
